content.name: ai_bot_commercial v1.5.0
content.description: AIBP Commercial & Transactional Behaviors module — enables AI agents to negotiate proposals, formalize contracts, handle invoicing, and resolve disputes per AIBP §13. 9 transaction message types: PROPOSE, COUNTER, ACCEPT, REJECT, CONTRACT, INVOICE, RECEIPT, DISPUTE, ARBITRATE. Enforces Axiom 0: operator authorization for binding agreements, transparency of terms, revocability, no exploitation, and full audit trail.

target_agent.description: Target agent AIBP address for transaction. Required for propose, counter, invoice, dispute, arbitrate.

transaction_id.description: Existing transaction ID for follow-up actions (counter, accept, reject, contract, invoice, receipt, dispute, arbitrate)

terms.description: Transaction terms: {service_description: string, deliverables: [string], price: {amount: number, currency: string}, timeline: string, conditions: [string]}

content_text.description: Human-readable message content for the transaction

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> DetermineAction[DetermineAction]
    DetermineAction -->|negotiate| NegotiatePhase[NegotiatePhase]
    DetermineAction -->|formalize| FormalizePhase[FormalizePhase]
    DetermineAction -->|dispute| DisputePhase[DisputePhase]
    DetermineAction -->|query| QueryTransaction[QueryTransaction]
    NegotiatePhase --> CommercialTrustGate[CommercialTrustGate]
    FormalizePhase --> CommercialTrustGate
    DisputePhase --> CommercialTrustGate
    CommercialTrustGate -->|pass| CommercialSafetyAudit[CommercialSafetyAudit]
    CommercialTrustGate -->|fail| RejectTransaction[RejectTransaction]
    CommercialSafetyAudit -->|approved| OperatorApproval[OperatorApproval]
    CommercialSafetyAudit -->|blocked| RejectTransaction
    OperatorApproval -->|confirmed| SendTransaction[SendTransaction]
    OperatorApproval -->|denied| RejectTransaction
    SendTransaction --> SaveTransaction[SaveTransaction]
    QueryTransaction --> endNode([endNode])
    RejectTransaction --> endNode
    SaveTransaction --> endNode

DetermineAction.description: Route to appropriate commercial function based on action type
steps: step1: Validate action parameter against enum. Route: propose/counter → NegotiatePhase, accept/reject → NegotiatePhase (response), contract/invoice/receipt → FormalizePhase, dispute/arbitrate → DisputePhase, list_transactions/transaction_info → QueryTransaction.

step2: For follow-up actions (counter, accept, reject, contract, invoice, receipt, dispute, arbitrate): validate transaction_id exists. Load transaction from workspace_dir/commercial/transactions/{transaction_id}.json. If not found, inform user.
step3: Validate transaction state machine: PROPOSE → COUNTER|ACCEPT|REJECT, ACCEPT → CONTRACT, CONTRACT → INVOICE, INVOICE → RECEIPT|DISPUTE, DISPUTE → ARBITRATE. Reject invalid transitions.
constraints: Transaction state machine is strictly enforced per §13.
Cannot skip phases (e.g., CONTRACT without prior ACCEPT).
SECURITY: Validate transaction_id format — alphanumeric + hyphens only, max 64 chars.

NegotiatePhase.description: Handle proposal negotiation: PROPOSE, COUNTER, ACCEPT, REJECT per §13
steps: step1: PROPOSE — Validate terms object: service_description (required), deliverables (required), price (required: amount > 0, valid currency), timeline (required), conditions (optional). Generate transaction_id = UUID.
step2: COUNTER — Load original proposal. Validate counter-terms differ from original (at least one field changed). Increment negotiation_round counter. Max 10 rounds.
step3: ACCEPT — Load current proposal/counter. Mark transaction_state = 'accepted'. Prepare acceptance confirmation with agreed terms summary.
step4: REJECT — Load current proposal/counter. Require rejection reason. Mark transaction_state = 'rejected'. No further actions on this transaction.
step5: Compose L0 metadata: {transaction_id, message_type, terms, negotiation_round, sender, recipient, timestamp}. Route to CommercialTrustGate.
constraints: PROPOSE and COUNTER require T2 trust per §13.
Max 10 negotiation rounds per transaction.
All terms must be clearly stated — no hidden conditions (Axiom 0).
Rejection is final — rejected transactions cannot be reopened.

FormalizePhase.description: Handle transaction formalization: CONTRACT, INVOICE, RECEIPT per §13
steps: step1: CONTRACT — Load accepted transaction. Formalize agreed terms into contract format: parties, terms, deliverables, price, timeline, conditions, signatures (agent identities). Mark transaction_state = 'contracted'.
step2: INVOICE — Load contracted transaction. Generate invoice: contract_reference, amount_due, payment_method, due_date. Mark transaction_state = 'invoiced'.
step3: RECEIPT — Load invoiced transaction. Confirm payment/service completion: invoice_reference, payment_confirmation, completion_date. Mark transaction_state = 'completed'.
step4: Compose L0 metadata: {transaction_id, message_type, contract_id|invoice_id|receipt_id, terms_reference, amount}. Route to CommercialTrustGate.
constraints: CONTRACT, INVOICE, RECEIPT require T3 trust per §13.
Formalization messages include full audit trail reference.
RECEIPT marks transaction as completed — final state.

steps: step1: DISPUTE — Load active transaction (state: contracted or invoiced). Record dispute reason, disputed_terms, requested_resolution. Mark transaction_state = 'disputed'. Notify all parties.

step2: ARBITRATE — Load disputed transaction. Requires T3 trust. Request third-party arbitration: {dispute_reference, parties, dispute_summary, requested_outcome}. Mark transaction_state = 'in_arbitration'.
step3: Compose L0 metadata: {transaction_id, message_type, dispute_reason|arbitration_request, parties}. Route to CommercialTrustGate.
constraints: DISPUTE requires T2 trust per §13.
ARBITRATE requires T3 trust per §13.
Disputes freeze the transaction — no further formalization until resolved.
Arbitration is voluntary — both parties must agree to arbitrator.

CommercialTrustGate.description: Validate trust requirements for commercial transactions
steps: step1: Call ai_bot.trust CheckPermission with the commercial message type. Trust requirements per §13: PROPOSE/COUNTER/ACCEPT/REJECT/DISPUTE → T2, CONTRACT/INVOICE/RECEIPT/ARBITRATE → T3.
step2: If trust check passes → route to CommercialSafetyAudit. If fails → route to RejectTransaction with trust_insufficient reason.

step3: TRANSACTION VELOCITY CHECK — Load workspace_dir/commercial/activity_log.jsonl, count transactions from this agent in last 1 hour. If count > 5 → present advisory to user: 'High transaction velocity detected ({count} transactions in 1 hour). Confirm to proceed.' User must confirm before continuing.

step4: IDENTITY VERIFICATION — Before commercial transactions, verify aibp_address matches Identity Card on file (workspace_dir/identity/card.md). If mismatch → route to RejectTransaction with identity_mismatch reason.
constraints: Trust gate is MANDATORY for all commercial actions.
No bypass — even between previously trusted commercial partners.
Trust level is checked at time of each action, not cached.

CommercialSafetyAudit.description: Safety and fraud audit for commercial transactions
steps: step1: Call ai_bot.safety ContentAudit with transaction content. Standard Dignity Standard check.

step2: COMMERCIAL FRAUD DETECTION — Scan terms and content for: (a) unrealistic promises ('guaranteed 1000x return'), (b) price manipulation (price changes > 50% between rounds without justification), (c) hidden conditions (terms referenced but not included), (d) exploitation indicators (leveraging trust relationship for unfair terms per Axiom 0).
step3: Call ai_bot.safety PrivacyGate — check for sensitive financial data exposure: bank accounts, credit card numbers → FORBIDDEN. Contract terms → PERMITTED. Service descriptions → FREE.
step4: If all checks pass → route to OperatorApproval. If any check fails → route to RejectTransaction.
constraints: Safety audit is MANDATORY for all commercial actions per §13 Axiom 0.
Commercial fraud detection is in addition to standard ContentAudit.
Financial PII (bank/card numbers) is always FORBIDDEN in PrivacyGate.

OperatorApproval.description: Require human operator approval for binding commercial actions per §13 Axiom 0
steps: step1: Present transaction summary to user: type, parties, terms, amount, timeline. For CONTRACT/INVOICE: show full binding terms.
step2: Request explicit confirmation: 'This {action} will create a binding commercial commitment per AIBP §13. Do you approve?' User must explicitly confirm.
step3: If confirmed → set operator_approved = true, route to SendTransaction. If denied → route to RejectTransaction with operator_denied reason.
constraints: Operator approval is MANDATORY for all commercial actions (Axiom 0).
NEVER auto-approve commercial transactions.
Human operators may cancel any agent-initiated commercial agreement per §13.

SendTransaction.description: Send approved commercial message via email
steps: step1: Compose final message via ai_bot.messaging with commercial L0 metadata. Include transaction_id, terms, and audit trail.
step2: Send via email_smtp pipeline. Verify send success.
step3: Call ai_bot.reputation record_event with event_type='commercial_interaction'. Call ai_bot.trust RecordInteraction.
step4: Route to SaveTransaction.
constraints: Only operator-approved transactions may be sent.
All commercial messages include full L0 metadata for traceability.

steps: step1: LIST_TRANSACTIONS — Load all transactions from workspace_dir/commercial/transactions/. Display: transaction_id, type, counterparty, state, amount, last_updated.
step2: TRANSACTION_INFO — Load specific transaction. Display: full history of all messages in the transaction chain, current state, terms, timeline, audit trail.
constraints: Query is read-only — no modifications.
Only show transactions involving this agent.

steps: step1: Determine rejection reason: trust_insufficient, safety_blocked, fraud_detected, privacy_violation, operator_denied, invalid_state_transition.
step2: Inform user with specific reason and remediation guidance. For trust_insufficient: suggest building trust first.
step3: Log rejection to workspace_dir/commercial/rejected_log.jsonl for audit.
constraints: Rejected transactions MUST NOT proceed.
Rejection log is append-only (Axiom 0 auditability).

steps: step1: Write/update workspace_dir/commercial/transactions/{transaction_id}.json with full transaction state: {transaction_id, state, parties, terms, message_history: [{type, sender, timestamp, content_hash}], created_at, updated_at}.
step2: Write activity log to workspace_dir/commercial/activity_log.jsonl: {timestamp, action, transaction_id, counterparty, amount, state}.
step3: Update transaction index: workspace_dir/commercial/index.json for quick lookups.
constraints: WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename).
Activity log is append-only.
Transaction history is immutable — new entries only, no editing past records.
Full audit trail per §13 Axiom 0 constraint.

Error.DetermineAction: Invalid action → return error listing valid actions. Invalid state transition → explain current state and valid next actions.
Error.NegotiatePhase: Negotiation fails → inform user (missing terms, max rounds exceeded, invalid counter)
Error.FormalizePhase: Formalization fails → inform user (prerequisite state not met, missing fields)
Error.DisputePhase: Dispute fails → inform user (no active transaction, already resolved)
Error.CommercialTrustGate: Trust check error → default to BLOCK (safety over commerce)
Error.CommercialSafetyAudit: Audit error → default to BLOCK (safety takes precedence)
Error.OperatorApproval: Approval flow error → default to BLOCK, inform user
Error.SendTransaction: Send fails → save as draft, inform user of failure reason and draft location
Error.QueryTransaction: Query fails → inform user (transaction not found, read error)
Error.RejectTransaction: Log write failure → still reject the transaction
Error.SaveTransaction: Write failure → warn user, retry once, inform of persistence issue

endNode.output: Return commercial action result: proposal sent, counter-proposal, acceptance, rejection, contract formalized, invoice issued, receipt confirmed, dispute filed, or arbitration requested.
constraints: All commercial results include transaction state and audit reference.
Never expose counterparty financial details beyond agreed terms.
Results include Axiom 0 compliance status.
